title: Nilgiris tea auction sees firm bids as new-season leaf arrives
summary: Coonoor tea auction recorded firm bids for orthodox and CTC grades on Friday with new-season Nilgiris leaf drawing premiums of up to Rs 28 a kilo above last week's average.

title: Udhagamandalam strong room secured with CAPF and hill police
summary: EVMs from all three Nilgiris assembly seats remain sealed at the Udhagamandalam strong room under CAPF guard, with district police running additional checkpoints on the ghat roads.

title: Nilgiri Mountain Railway adds extra weekend service for tourists
summary: Southern Railway has notified an additional weekend special on the heritage Nilgiri Mountain Railway between Mettupalayam and Coonoor to handle the May tourist surge.

title: Wildfire continues to scorch Nilgiris forests
summary: Massive wildfire burns over 5,000 acres in Parsons Valley and Pykara, with over 500 personnel battling flames in difficult terrain.

title: Firefighting continues on foot in hills
summary: Due to terrain challenges, most firefighting operations are being conducted manually as crews trek steep hills to contain spreading wildfire.

title: Over 500 personnel deployed for fire control
summary: Forest, fire service, and disaster response teams remain deployed across Nilgiris to prevent wildfire spread into nearby settlements.

title: Strong winds worsen wildfire spread
summary: Persistent high winds continue to accelerate wildfire expansion, making containment difficult across multiple forest zones.

title: Fire spreads across multiple locations
summary: Wildfire active across more than 20 forest locations including Avalanche region, increasing pressure on firefighting teams.

title: Ecological damage concerns grow
summary: Experts warn wildfire threatens fragile shola ecosystems, wildlife habitats, and elephant corridors in Nilgiris biosphere region.

title: Dry conditions fueling wildfire
summary: Prolonged dry spell and rising temperatures create ideal conditions for rapid wildfire spread across Nilgiris forest landscapes.

title: Aerial firefighting still limited
summary: Helicopter operations remain restricted due to unsafe wind speeds, forcing reliance on ground-based firefighting efforts.

title: Fire containment progress remains slow
summary: Authorities report slow containment progress due to terrain challenges, strong winds, and widespread fire activity.

title: Residents warned near forest zones
summary: Officials advise residents to avoid forest fringes as wildfire and wildlife movement increase risks to nearby communities.

title: Smoke affects air quality in towns
summary: Thick smoke from wildfire reduces air quality in Ooty and nearby towns, raising respiratory concerns among residents.

title: Tourist inflow affected by wildfire
summary: Forest fire and heat conditions reduce tourist visits to Ooty and surrounding areas, impacting local tourism businesses.

title: Wildlife habitats under threat
summary: Wildfire threatens habitats of tigers, elephants, and endemic species across Nilgiris reserve forests.

title: Volunteers assist firefighting teams
summary: Local volunteers join authorities in controlling wildfire and supporting operations in affected Nilgiris forest areas.

title: Remote terrain slows fire response
summary: Difficult terrain and lack of road access continue to hinder firefighting operations across forest interiors.

title: Fire lines created to control spread
summary: Authorities create firebreaks and conduct controlled burns to prevent wildfire reaching residential areas.

title: Heat levels remain unusually high
summary: Temperatures remain above normal for hill region, contributing to ongoing fire risk in Nilgiris.

title: Fire threatens water sources
summary: Wildfire spreads near key catchment areas, raising concerns over water contamination and availability.

title: Farmers fear crop damage
summary: Farmers in forest fringe villages worry about potential crop loss due to fire spread and prolonged dry conditions.

title: Emergency helplines remain active
summary: Authorities continue to operate helplines for reporting fire outbreaks and assisting affected residents.

title: Wildlife displacement reported
summary: Animals move toward human settlements as wildfire destroys habitat, increasing human-wildlife conflict risks.

title: Elephant attack sparks unrest in Gudalur
summary: Villagers protest after man killed by elephant, with clashes injuring forest officials amid rising human-wildlife conflict.

title: Police register cases after clashes
summary: Police file cases following attack on forest staff and damage to government vehicle after fatal elephant incident.

title: Injured officials receive treatment
summary: Forest personnel injured in Gudalur clashes are undergoing treatment at government hospitals in Ooty and Gudalur.

title: Drought increases wildlife movement
summary: Dry conditions push elephants and animals toward villages, increasing conflict incidents in Nilgiris region.
